title: Diagnose 'passes alone, fails after X' — three root causes
prompt: Write `docs/passes_alone_fails_after.md` for this scenario: `mix test test/my_app/feature_test.exs` passes. `mix test` fails the same test. The failing test is the LAST test in module `MyApp.FeatureTest`. Enumerate 3 plausible root causes in order of likelihood: (1) a test in a PRIOR module (any module) leaked a process that held a sandbox connection, (2) a prior test wrote to `Application.put_env` or ETS and didn't clean up, (3) a prior test left an Oban job in the DB via `Oban.insert!` (not valid for sandbox but some projects do it). For each, give a diagnostic step using `mix test --seed` or `--trace`. Must state 'do NOT disable async as the first step'.
research_citation: "It's impossible to know without seeing your code but this generally happens because one test is causing persisting side effects" — Elixir Forum, sodapopcan
